steps.expected: Page loads with all UI components
steps.observed: Page loaded successfully with navigation, controls, registers, flags, memory view, and code editor visible

steps.action: Navigate to simulator, observe register panel
steps.expected: All 8086 registers displayed with hex values
steps.observed: 16-bit registers visible: AX=0000, BX=0000, CX=0000, DX=0000, SI=0000, DI=0000, BP=0000, SP=FFFE, CS=0000, DS=0000, ES=0000, IP=0000. 8-bit registers visible: AL=00, AH=00, BL=00, BH=00, CL=00, CH=00, DL=00, DH=00

evidence.textContent: Registers panel shows all 8086 registers with hex values in 'Ready' state

assertions.title: CPU Flags Display with Boolean States

steps.action: Navigate to simulator, observe flags panel
steps.expected: CPU flags displayed with boolean states (0/1)
steps.observed: Status Flags: CF=0, PF=0, AF=0, ZF=0, SF=0. Control Flags: TF=0, IF=1, DF=0, OF=0. Flag descriptions shown on hover/display.

evidence.textContent: Flags panel shows all 8 CPU flags with boolean states and descriptions

assertions.title: Step Button Executes One Instruction

steps.expected: Step button enabled, clicking executes one instruction
steps.observed: Step button enabled after loading preset. Clicking Step shows 'Execution ErrorUnknown instruction: MSG' - simulator attempts to execute data definition 'MSG DB ...' as instruction instead of skipping to code segment
Step shows 'Execution ErrorUnknown instruction: RESULT' - data definition treated as instruction
Step shows 'Executing:MAIN PROC' - label treated as instruction
Step shows 'Execution ErrorUnknown instruction: TEMP' - data definition treated as instruction
Step shows 'Execution ErrorUnknown instruction: COUNT' - data definition treated as instruction

evidence.textContent: All 14 example presets contain .DATA segments. Simulator does not properly skip .DATA segment when executing - it attempts to execute data definitions as instructions.

assertions.issues: Simulator bug: Cannot execute Step on any example program because all examples contain .DATA segments that the simulator incorrectly attempts to execute as code. The simulator does not properly parse and skip .DATA segment definitions.

assertions.title: Run Button Executes Continuously, Halt Stops

steps.observed: Cannot verify - Step execution fails due to simulator bug with .DATA segments

assertions.issues: Blocked by P5-SIM-004 failure - cannot test continuous execution when single-step execution is broken

assertions.title: Breakpoint Support

steps.expected: User can set/clear breakpoints, execution pauses at breakpoint
steps.observed: Cannot verify - Step execution fails preventing breakpoint testing

assertions.issues: Blocked by P5-SIM-004 failure - cannot test breakpoints without working execution

steps.observed: Dropdown opened showing 14 presets: HelloWorld.asm, Template.asm, Addition.asm, Buff2.asm, Buffered.asm, demo.asm, proc.asm, forloop.asm, ifelse.asm, Exchange.asm, Compare.asm, RegisterSwap.asm, ArithmeticDemo.asm, whileloop.asm

steps.expected: Code loads in editor, registers initialize
steps.observed: Code loaded into editor (12 instructions), 'HelloWorld.asm' shown as selected, Step button enabled, registers initialized to 0000, IP=0000
Code loaded (13 instructions), registers reset to 0000

evidence.textContent: Dropdown shows '14 presets available'. Selecting preset loads code into editor and initializes all registers to 0.

assertions.title: Memory View Shows Addresses and Hex Values

steps.action: Observe memory panel on simulator page
steps.expected: Memory view shows addresses (DS:0000) and hex values
steps.observed: Memory panel visible with: DS:0000 base address, Addr column (0123456789ABCDEF), Hex view showing 16 bytes per row, ASCII representation. Navigation buttons: -256, -16, +16, +256. Display toggle: Hex/Dec buttons.

evidence.textContent: Memory view shows DS:0000 through DS:00FF with hex values and ASCII representation. Memory updates after execution could not be verified due to execution bug.

assertions.issues: Memory view UI is present and functional, but updates after execution could not be verified (blocked by execution bug)

assertions.title: Code Highlights Current Instruction

steps.action: Load preset, observe current instruction indicator
steps.expected: Current instruction highlighted in code editor
steps.observed: Text shows 'Current instruction at line 8 Executing:MSG DB ...' but the line numbers displayed in the code editor (1-12) don't match the 'line 8' referenced. The simulator appears to use a different line numbering than what's displayed in the code editor. Cannot verify highlighting works because execution is broken.

evidence.textContent: 'Current instruction at line 8' shown but code editor shows 12 instructions numbered 1-12. Execution state shows 'Executing:MSG DB ...' which is a data definition, not an instruction.

assertions.issues: Cannot verify if highlighting works correctly because: 1) The simulator has a bug executing programs with .DATA segments, 2) Line number reference doesn't match displayed code editor line numbers

assertions.title: Speed Control Slider

steps.action: Observe speed control on simulator page
steps.expected: Speed control slider present with Fast/Slow labels
steps.observed: Slider labeled 'Adjust execution speed' with 'Fast' and 'Slow' labels. Default shows 'Speed:100ms delay'. Slider is interactable (ref=e17).

evidence.textContent: Speed control slider present with 100ms delay default

assertions.issues: Slider UI is present but actual speed adjustment during execution could not be verified due to execution bug

frictions.description: Simulator dropdown closes frequently when trying to select different examples - requires reopening dropdown multiple times
frictions.resolution: Worked around by taking snapshots before each click to track ref numbers

frictions.description: Simulator has a fundamental bug where it attempts to execute .DATA segment definitions as instructions - all 14 example presets contain .DATA segments
frictions.resolution: None - this is a simulator implementation bug

blockers.description: Simulator cannot execute any example program because all contain .DATA segments that the simulator incorrectly treats as executable instructions. This blocks testing of Step (P5-SIM-004), Run (P5-SIM-005), Breakpoint (P5-SIM-006), Memory updates (P5-SIM-008), Instruction highlighting (P5-SIM-009), and Speed control verification (P5-SIM-010).
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tested 5 different example presets (HelloWorld.asm, Template.asm, Addition.asm, Exchange.asm, forloop.asm) - all exhibit the same bug where .DATA definitions are treated as instructions

summary: Tested 10 assertions for P5-SIM (Simulator). 4 passed (SIM-001, SIM-002, SIM-003, SIM-007), 2 failed (SIM-004, SIM-009), 4 blocked (SIM-005, SIM-006, SIM-008, SIM-010). Root cause: Simulator has a bug where it attempts to execute .DATA segment definitions as instructions. All 14 example presets contain .DATA segments, making it impossible to test any execution-related functionality. The register display, flags display, memory view UI, preset dropdown, and speed slider are all present and functional. The execution engine needs to be fixed to properly parse and skip .DATA segments before executing code.
